sentence_reviews.text: Policies are optimized directly, often with a learned value or critic to reduce variance and guide improvement.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto RL book, policy-gradient and actor-critic method chapters, including value-function baselines/critics.
sentence_reviews.notes: The definition matches standard policy-gradient and actor-critic material in the cited source.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives is to connect Reinforcement learning, Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives to downstream capabilities including Reward-model training, RLHF & RLAIF, Vision–language–action models, Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO).
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto RL book, policy-gradient and actor-critic chapters; packet sources do not include direct RLHF, VLA, or GRPO evidence.
sentence_reviews.notes: The RL and value-objective connections are supported, but several downstream capabilities are not directly supported by the cited packet sources.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Reward-model training, RLHF & RLAIF
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto RL book, policy-gradient/actor-critic methods; no packet source directly covering RLHF/RLAIF reward-model training.
sentence_reviews.notes: Policy optimization is relevant to RLHF, but the packet does not cite an RLHF/RLAIF source for this node.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto RL book, general policy optimization material; no packet source specifically addresses vision-language-action models.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited evidence does not directly support the VLA downstream example.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto RL book, policy-gradient methods; no packet source specifically addresses GRPO.
sentence_reviews.notes: The packet sources do not establish GRPO or its relation to actor-critic objectives.

sentence_reviews.text: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Reinforcement learning, Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto RL book, policy-gradient and actor-critic objective material.
sentence_reviews.notes: The dependencies are supported; the generic objective-mismatch limitation is not directly sourced.

sentence_reviews.text: Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives uses Reinforcement learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto RL book, policy-gradient and actor-critic chapters.
sentence_reviews.notes: Policy-gradient and actor-critic objectives are directly RL methods.

sentence_reviews.text: Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto RL book, actor-critic methods with critic/value-function learning and TD-style updates.
sentence_reviews.notes: Actor-critic methods use value/critic estimates, often TD-learned; the exact 'optimizes_for' rationale is atlas wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto RL book, policy-gradient optimization methods.
sentence_reviews.notes: Policy-gradient methods directly optimize policy parameters.

edge_reviews.notes: Policy-gradient and actor-critic methods are core RL methods.
Actor-critic methods commonly use value/TD critics, but 'optimizes_for' is less precise than a uses/evaluated-with relation.
Direct policy optimization supports the edge and common strength.
